question: A worker uses `zeebe.client.message.time-to-live=PT10M` on Camunda 8.8. Which replacement property does the migration table point to?

a.text: The `zeebe.client.message.*` table maps `zeebe.client.message.time-to-live` to `camunda.client.message-time-to-live` (env `CAMUNDA_CLIENT_MESSAGETIMETOLIVE`) — flattened, not nested under a `message.` sub-key.

b.text: The new property name flattens the `.message.` segment; `camunda.client.message.time-to-live` (with the dot) is not the form the docs list.

c.text: Message TTL is a client-level concern, not a worker default; it does not live under `worker.defaults.*` in the new namespace.

d.text: There is no `camunda.client.ttl.*` group on the page; the docs reuse a single flat key `message-time-to-live`.

explanation: The deprecated `zeebe.client.message.time-to-live` is replaced by the flat property `camunda.client.message-time-to-live`. Note the migration drops the `.message.` segment in favour of the hyphenated key. The sibling `max-message-size` is migrated the same way to `camunda.client.max-message-size`.

docs.title: Camunda Spring Boot starter properties reference
